title: Character Palette Definition
description:
Per-character palette swap definition. Declares the canonical (source) atlas colour slots a character's sprite sheet is authored with, and the 8 named target variants the hue-shift batch script (M1.5) and the runtime palette-swap shader use to recolour that sheet.

The Seed contract requires '8 manual palette swaps per character via hue-shift batch script' — one of these files lives per character and is the single source of truth that pipeline reads. Slot names ('body', 'accent', 'highlight') match the PaletteSlot union in src/characters/paletteSwapShader.ts; canonical (variant index 0) target colours match the corresponding entry in src/characters/palettes.ts so the runtime TS table and the JSON pipeline data never drift.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference. Editors use this for IntelliSense; the runtime ignores it.

characterId.description: Character id this palette file applies to. Mirrors CharacterId in src/types — a new character means a new enum entry here AND a new <char>.json file.

displayName.description: Human-readable character name used by tooling output ('Generated 8 variants for Wolf').

source.description: Canonical source-palette slot colours present in the character's authored sprite sheet (palette index 0). The hue-shift batch script scans the input PNG for these exact RGB values and substitutes them per variant.

highlight.description: Optional bright highlight pixels (eyes, fur sheen). May equal accent when no separate highlight slot exists in the source art.

variants.description: Exactly 8 named target variants, indexed 0..7. Index 0 is canonical (its target colours equal source colours). Index 0..7 must appear in ascending order with no gaps.

rgbHex.description: RGB colour in #RRGGBB hex notation. Lowercase preferred for diff stability. Alpha is implicit (always 0xFF) — palette swaps preserve the source pixel's alpha.

index.description: Variant index 0..7. Mirrors PlayerSlot.paletteIndex and the character-select cycler. Must equal the array position of this entry.

name.description: Short human-readable swatch label ('Crimson', 'Sky', 'Mint'). Surfaced in the character-select tooltip and the accessibility readout.

isCanonical.description: Marks the canonical variant whose target colours equal the source colours. Exactly one variant per file should be canonical, and it must be index 0. Optional — defaults to false.

mappings.description: Per-slot source→target colour pair. The hue-shift script walks every pixel of the canonical atlas; pixels matching mappings[slot].from are rewritten to mappings[slot].to to produce this variant's PNG. Pixels matching no slot pass through unchanged so transparent edges and shared neutrals stay intact.

from.description: Source pixel colour to match. Must equal the corresponding source.<slot> colour at the file root for the canonical variant; for non-canonical variants must still equal the file-root source.<slot> (the hue-shift script always reads pixels from the canonical atlas, never from a previously-generated variant).

to.description: Target pixel colour to write. Free to differ across variants — this is what produces the visible palette swap.
